questions.prompt: Why use the RGB space-separated format for CSS variables (e.g., `--primary: 59 130 246`)?

choices.text: Lets you use Tailwind's alpha modifier syntax. Define the variable as space-separated channels (`--primary: 59 130 246`), reference it in tailwind.config as `'rgb(var(--primary) / <alpha-value>)'`. Now `bg-primary/50` works (50% opacity), `bg-primary/10` works (subtle background tints), etc. Without this format, alpha modifiers fail because Tailwind can't inject the alpha value into a pre-formatted rgb() string. HSL space-separated (`217 91% 60%`) works the same way. The single-color-with-alpha pattern is essential for theme-aware tints (overlays, hover backgrounds, focus rings).

questions.explanation: RGB space-separated enables Tailwind's `/alpha` modifier syntax. Otherwise no opacity tints.

questions.prompt: Light theme briefly flashes before dark theme on page load. Fix?

choices.text: Use `next-themes` (for Next.js) which handles SSR-safe theme detection — reads the stored preference or OS setting server-side and applies the class to <html> before React hydrates. No flash. For custom solutions: add an inline script in <head> that reads localStorage and the `prefers-color-scheme` media query and sets the `.dark` class on documentElement BEFORE the rest of the page loads. The script runs synchronously (blocking; tiny cost) so the class is on the element before any CSS evaluates — page renders directly in the correct theme. `suppressHydrationWarning` on <html> needed to prevent React's hydration warning about the class mismatch.

questions.explanation: next-themes or an inline script that sets the class before React loads. Eliminates FOUC.
